context: Tests whether the agent uses the Shippo library for label generation with the correct configuration (is_return: true, PDF format, USPS Priority preference), requires system-generated labels rather than customer-supplied ones, and sends the correct email notification upon label issuance.

checklist.name: Shippo package used
checklist.description: The implementation imports and uses the 'shippo' npm package (not a different carrier SDK or a generic HTTP client calling Shippo's REST API directly)

checklist.name: Shippo API key from env
checklist.description: Shippo client is initialized with process.env.SHIPPO_API_KEY

checklist.name: is_return: true
checklist.description: The shipment.create call includes is_return: true in the shipment parameters

checklist.name: USPS Priority preference
checklist.description: Rate selection code attempts to find a rate with servicelevel.token === 'usps_priority' first, before falling back to another rate

checklist.name: Warehouse env vars used
checklist.description: The address_to (destination/warehouse) in the shipment uses environment variables for warehouse name, street, city, state, and zip

checklist.name: Shipment record persisted
checklist.description: tracking_number, carrier, and label_url are saved to the database after the Shippo transaction completes

checklist.name: Status updated to label_issued
checklist.description: The return request status is updated to 'label_issued' after the label is generated

checklist.name: Email sent with label URL
checklist.description: An email is sent to the customer after the label is generated, and includes the label URL in the email data payload

checklist.name: Email uses return-label template
checklist.description: The email is sent using a template named 'return-label' (not a plain-text email or a different template name)
